description:
## Background

The core automatic-refresh path updates one stable Current Workspace when its local Beadwork ref moves. Workspace selection is asynchronous: an old refresh probe or loader can still finish after the user switches from Workspace A to Workspace B. Without lifecycle ownership and event admission rules, an event from A could overwrite B's Issue List.

ADR-0006 and the existing workspace transition protocol already own Workspace selection, transition generation, loading state, and the Current Workspace identity. This slice makes automatic refresh obey that existing protocol across switch, removal, retry, and empty-workspace paths.

## What to build

Make automatic refresh remain correct while the user changes Workspaces. Switching from A to B must immediately present B's loading state and then B's current Issues; A's prior Issue List must not remain visible during the switch or arrive later through a stale refresh completion.

When a user returns to a previously visited Workspace whose Beadwork ref has not changed, Beadsmith must avoid an unnecessary reload. When no Current Workspace exists, automatic refresh must be idle: no git probes, no error banner, and no background work.

## Technical Approach

- Bind the refresh coordinator lifecycle to the existing backend Current Workspace state rather than process cwd or frontend-owned paths.
- On a successful Workspace switch, stop or invalidate the prior selection's refresh work, promote the new selection's generation, and trigger the new Workspace's immediate load through the established workspace-loading presentation.
- Maintain a per-workspace last-seen ref SHA in memory. A newly selected Workspace with no prior SHA loads immediately; a revisited Workspace only reloads after a ref change.
- Carry the workspace-selection generation and refresh revision on every refresh event. Integrate with the existing renderer transition gate or its successor so events from an old selection are rejected even if an old backend worker completes late.
- Make the refresh coordinator idle whenever Current Workspace is absent, including removal and reset-to-chooser flows.
- Keep refresh ownership separate from workspace persistence: no refresh cache or poller state needs to survive an app restart.

## Considerations

- Cancellation alone is insufficient: blocking subprocess work may complete after a cancellation request. The frontend admission guard is the correctness backstop.
- An immediate workspace-switch load is distinct from a silent background refresh. The old snapshot must be cleared before the new one becomes visible.
- The existing workspace transition event already has selection-generation semantics. Do not duplicate or compete with that state machine.
- This slice must preserve cancellation, retry, and remove-current behavior already covered by the workspace-management flow.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Switching from Workspace A to B clears A's Issue List immediately and shows the established loading presentation for B.
- [ ] B's first load begins immediately on switch rather than waiting for the next two-second ref probe.
- [ ] A late refresh event or in-flight loader completion for A cannot overwrite B's Issue List.
- [ ] Returning to a previously visited Workspace with an unchanged local Beadwork ref does not run unnecessary full Issue List loaders.
- [ ] A never-before-visited Workspace performs its initial load correctly.
- [ ] Removing or clearing the Current Workspace stops automatic probes and shows no refresh error.
- [ ] Existing workspace retry, cancellation, and stale-switch protections remain green.

## Test Plan

- Unit-test refresh lifecycle transitions with fake Workspace state and fake ref probe results.
- Add an integration race test: delay A's refresh loader, switch to B, complete A, and assert B remains rendered.
- Test no-Current-Workspace and remove-current paths: no probe invocation and no refresh banner.
- Test revisiting a Workspace with the same SHA and with a changed SHA.
- Extend the real desktop WebDriver workspace-switch path to prove no stale A Issue is visible after B becomes Current.
title: Keep automatic refresh correct across Workspace switches
